should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator for customer onboarding that handles documents, human approvals, tool calls, SLA timers, exception paths, and post-launch monitoring.
Our incident response agent workflow repeats every week but has too many handoffs and delays. Model the as-is process, instrument it, and propose a redesign.
Convert this support workflow into a BPMN-like execution model with events, tasks, gateways, lanes, escalations, and audit evidence.
We need process metrics for a claims-handling orchestrator: cycle time, waiting time, rework loops, queue buildup, and which variants cause SLA misses.
should_not_trigger: Make a simple three-step checklist for manually publishing a static blog post.
Explain what a BPMN gateway is in one paragraph without designing a workflow.
Choose between two database indexes for a single query optimization task.
edge_cases: The team has a one-off migration plan with several dependencies and approvals. Decide whether BPM lifecycle modeling is useful or whether a dependency graph plus checklist is enough.
We have no event logs, only anecdotes about slow approvals. Start with process discovery and instrumentation before recommending automation.
A workflow has BPMN terms in the prompt, but it is only a notation question. Trigger only if the user needs orchestration design, execution, monitoring, or redesign.
